title: festuca ovina var. grandiflora
intro: Festuca ovina var. grandiflora, also known as sheep's fescue, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of sheep's fescue include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to support respiratory health. Sheep's fescue can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
